annotations.label: Finite group

anchor.snippet: a finite group is a group whose underlying set is finite

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Group` and expresses finiteness via a `Finite` (or `Fintype`) instance, together giving the notion of a finite group.

annotations.label: Symmetric group S_n

anchor.snippet: is the group whose elements are all the permutations of the

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm α` is the group of permutations of `α`; taking `α = Fin n` gives Sₙ.

annotations.label: Order of symmetric group

anchor.snippet: it follows that the order (the number of elements) of the symmetric group

annotations.note: `Fintype.card_perm` proves `Fintype.card (Perm α) = (Fintype.card α)!`.

annotations.label: Cyclic group Z_n

anchor.snippet: is a group all of whose elements are powers of a particular element

annotations.note: `IsCyclic` is the predicate that a group is generated by a single element; the concrete Z_n is `ZMod n`.

annotations.label: Complex nth roots of unity realization

anchor.snippet: A typical realization of this group is as the complex

annotations.note: `rootsOfUnity k R` defines the group of nth roots of unity in a commutative ring, and `rootsOfUnity.isCyclic` shows this group is cyclic in an integral domain.

annotations.label: Cyclic group is isomorphic to nth roots of unity

anchor.snippet: Sending a to a primitive root of unity gives an isomorphism between the two

annotations.note: This lemma constructs the isomorphism between a finite cyclic group and the roots of unity in a suitable ring.

annotations.label: Abelian group

anchor.snippet: is a group in which the result of applying the group operation to two group elements does not depend on their order

annotations.note: `CommGroup` (and `AddCommGroup`) are Mathlib's typeclasses for abelian groups.

annotations.label: Structure of finite abelian groups

anchor.snippet: An arbitrary finite abelian group is isomorphic to a direct sum of finite cyclic groups of prime power order

annotations.note: This states any finite abelian group is a direct sum of `ZMod (p^n)` factors, i.e. the primary decomposition theorem.

annotations.label: Group of Lie type

anchor.snippet: A group of Lie type is a group closely related to the group

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general notion of a group of Lie type / Chevalley group; only specific matrix groups like `SpecialLinearGroup` exist.

annotations.label: Jordan's simplicity of PSL(2,q)

anchor.snippet: theorem that the projective special linear group

annotations.note: No simplicity theorem for PSL(2, q) appears in Mathlib (loogle finds no `PSL.*simple` declarations).

annotations.label: Lagrange's theorem

anchor.snippet: the order (number of elements) of every subgroup

annotations.note: `Subgroup.card_subgroup_dvd_card` gives Lagrange's theorem: `Nat.card H ∣ Nat.card G`.

annotations.label: Sylow theorems

anchor.snippet: This provides a partial converse to Lagrange's theorem giving information about how many subgroups of a given order are contained in

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Sylow` file contains all three Sylow theorems: existence (`exists_subgroup_card_pow_prime`), conjugacy (`isPretransitive_of_finite`), and count (`card_sylow_modEq_one`).

annotations.label: Cayley's theorem

anchor.snippet: states that every group G is isomorphic to a subgroup of the symmetric group acting on

annotations.note: Its docstring explicitly identifies it as "Cayley's theorem", giving the isomorphism `G ≃* range (toPermHom G G)`.

annotations.label: Burnside's theorem

anchor.snippet: Burnside's theorem in group theory states that if

annotations.note: Wikipedia refers to Burnside's `p^a q^b` solvability theorem; Mathlib only has Burnside's normal `p`-complement (transfer) theorem, which is a different result.

annotations.label: Non-abelian simple groups have order divisible by three primes

anchor.snippet: Hence each non-Abelian finite simple group has order divisible by at least three distinct primes

annotations.note: This corollary of Burnside's `p^a q^b` theorem is not in Mathlib, since its parent theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Feit–Thompson theorem

anchor.snippet: states that every finite group of odd order is solvable

annotations.note: The Feit–Thompson odd-order theorem is not formalized in Mathlib (grep finds no matching declarations).

annotations.label: Classification of finite simple groups

anchor.snippet: is a theorem stating that every finite simple group belongs to one of the following families

annotations.note: The CFSG is not in Mathlib; individual families (cyclic of prime order, alternating) are known simple but no unifying classification theorem exists.

annotations.label: Jordan–Hölder theorem

anchor.snippet: The Jordan–Hölder theorem is a more precise way of stating this fact about finite groups

annotations.note: Mathlib proves Jordan–Hölder abstractly for any `JordanHolderLattice`, which specializes to the group-theoretic statement.

annotations.label: Groups of prime order are cyclic

anchor.section: Number of groups of a given order
anchor.snippet: Every group of prime order is cyclic

annotations.note: `isCyclic_of_prime_card` shows a finite group of prime cardinality is cyclic.

annotations.label: Groups of order p^2

anchor.section: Number of groups of a given order
anchor.snippet: If n is the square of a prime, then there are exactly two possible isomorphism types of group of order

annotations.note: Mathlib proves a group of order p² is commutative, but the sharper `exactly two isomorphism types` classification is not present.

annotations.label: Groups of order pq are cyclic

anchor.section: Number of groups of a given order
anchor.snippet: every group of order pq is cyclic when

annotations.note: No lemma stating groups of order `pq` (with `p ∤ q-1`) are cyclic is present; loogle/grep yield no matches.

annotations.label: Squarefree order implies solvable

anchor.section: Number of groups of a given order
anchor.snippet: If n is squarefree , then any group of order n is solvable

annotations.note: `IsZGroup.of_squarefree` combined with `IsZGroup.instIsSolvableOfFinite` yields solvability of any finite group of squarefree order.

annotations.label: At most two simple groups of each order

anchor.section: Number of groups of a given order
anchor.snippet: For any positive integer n there are at most two simple groups of order

annotations.note: This deep result depends on CFSG and is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Infinitely many orders with two non-isomorphic simple groups

anchor.section: Number of groups of a given order
anchor.snippet: there are infinitely many positive integers n for which there are two non-isomorphic simple groups of order

annotations.note: No such existence-of-coincidences statement about simple groups is formalized in Mathlib.
